question.text: What is the threshold for triggering a mandatory open offer under SEBI (SAST) Regulations, 2011?

answer.text: Acquisition of 25% or more voting rights in a company
Acquisition of 15% or more voting rights in a company
Acquisition of 51% or more voting rights in a company
Acquisition of 10% or more voting rights in a company

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under Regulation 3(1) of SEBI (SAST) Regulations, 2011, any acquirer who acquires shares or voting rights entitling 25% or more voting rights must make a public announcement of an open offer. For companies listed on the Innovators Growth Platform, the threshold is 49%. **The Trap:** The 5% threshold under Regulation 3(2) applies to additional acquisitions within a financial year when holding is already above 25%. The initial trigger is 25%. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Two triggers: (1) Initial 25% threshold, (2) Additional 5% in any financial year if already holding 25-75%. Both require mandatory open offer.
